So the accountability challenge, I just want to give you a high-level overview. It's a very straightforward challenge. The intention here is that we use this as a holiday accountability challenge. Because that's where most gym stuff, the worst attrition is the last week of November, first week, week and a half of December, is where they get all the cancellations and freezes and the, hey, I'll be back in January type spiel. And so you can run an internal play to actively combat that. And so one of the goals here is to, the goal here is to save potential EFTs that would be, might potentially leave. And so the intention is not to make a ton of cash out of it, you make some money, but it's not going to be like a normal internal play that you might make five, ten grand on. And so the hook is three-pronged. First obviously you're providing an accountability challenge, which will be fun and group of atmosphere, all that kind of stuff, right? The second is that you're going to donate half or more of the revenue that comes in to Toys for Tots, right? And the other half of the revenue that comes in, you'll put straight towards a badass Christmas party at the end. Now you can choose to put all of it towards it or some of it, it's up to you. But this is definitely a gift. This is like just like a good, just put some good will into the community type play. Now from a promotional standpoint, you'll read, like make sure you read the copy in the emails because like it's aggressive, it's funny. It's like if you don't sign up for this, you must hate children. It's funny, but you can adjust it how you want so that it sounds like it's coming from you. But with the charity angle, the whole goal here is to get every single person signed up. And I would say that is your intention is like, hey guys, I want everyone to sign up for this. It's 50 bucks, half of it goes to kids who don't have Christmas presents and the other half goes to a badass party, which you're only invited to if you do it. So I want everyone to do it. I think it'd be cool for us as a community to rally together. And on top of that, you're going to give them the actual accountability challenge. So like I said, you could say it's 49 or you could say it's 99 or you could even say it's 49 to 99 meaning, hey, listen, $25 or your thing is going to go towards the party and then anything above that is going to go to Toys for Tots, minimum 50 bucks, right? And plenty of people sign for that because they just feel bad and you'll get some pretty decent sized donations typically. Every time I ran a play, I would always have a couple people drop a brand just because they're that kind of, you know, that's hopefully that's the environment you have at your facility. So that's the donation in the event. Do make it a sick party, which is cool. Training commission should be zero because it's just a good cause and they don't really have to do anything besides like make a funny video really and time people have to be on their team for the accountability challenge, right? So from a fulfillment standpoint, the easiest thing to do is put a poster board on the wall with the kind of challenge of all the names of the people, put a sticker next to their name or they will put a sticker next to their name each time they do a workout and take a sweaty selfie and check in on Facebook. So they'll take a picture, they'll check on Facebook with that picture and then that is how it counts, right? And ideally you want them to have multiple people in the picture and the beautiful thing about that is that if you have all these clients taking all these sweaty selfies, you're going to get great marketing material that you can use for ads, etc. Okay. Now you can double check these if you want to, but it's like why, right? And the winners are just going to get either some prizes that you can take some of the $25 to put towards or you can have silly prizes, which I would recommend like spray painted dumbbells, you know, things of that nature. You'll notice if you sign up for the accountability challenge and both teams have a perfect score, it's really, really unlikely. But you would just then have weight loss be the deciding factor. So you're like, hey guys, like, I'm going to hold you accountable and working out and eating well during this whole thing. And but it's really going to be around getting them in the gym because that's what's going to hold them accountable. And ultimately it's going to be the thing that keeps the retention, which is the goal. So I hope that makes sense. These are the prizes. It's very straightforward of a play. It has three kind of benefits as charity. It has the event at the end. It'll also be a pretty big referral generator because you give them $49 for their friend to sign up and they actually get the whole month for that donation, basically. So you should be able to sign those and flip those straight into EFTs. Like when they come in, I would have them put the $49 down and then have them sign up straight for the EFT that starts the next month. And if they ask you, do I have to do that? Like that's just always how we've done it and just give them that response and it works like almost every time. So I hope this makes sense. It's very straightforward. And yeah, enjoy. Use it as your holiday promotion because this way we can just not, we can avoid the sometimes the freeze frenzy that can sometimes happen in the November beginning of December.
